title: Kenkendra

jokes.text: Cheryl too good for you! And Ted Danson. He's handsome and flexible.
jokes.setup: Chunhua complimenting Cheryl during massage
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to insulting Larry and praising Ted Danson's flexibility
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb setup of casual brutality delivered by supporting character. The Ted Danson reference adds randomness that works.

jokes.text: Don't be jealous! You brittle like wood! You are so skinny. Why you no eat? Your skin so dry. You drink water? Not enough!
jokes.setup: Larry tries to deflect jealousy comment
jokes.punchline: Chunhua launches into brutal physical assessment of Larry's body
jokes.explanation: Escalating assault on Larry's physical state delivered with perfect directness. The accumulation makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You want happy ending? No, I don't... Happy end... No. You've only done 35 minutes. I paid for an hour. No! No, no, no, no. I don't want a happy ending! No! No, no happy ending! I don't want a happy ending! I just want an ending!
jokes.setup: Larry asks if massage is finished
jokes.punchline: Massive misunderstanding about 'happy ending' vs just wanting the massage to conclude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Curb miscommunication that escalates beautifully. Larry's increasingly frantic denials while trying to explain he just wants his time's worth is brilliant wordplay.

jokes.text: No. A hunch is something that's shared. Otherwise, it's just a thought in your head. A hunch is like a sneeze, gotta let that shit out.
jokes.setup: Larry says Jeff kept his hunch to himself
jokes.punchline: Leon's philosophical distinction about hunches needing to be shared, comparing to sneezes
jokes.explanation: Leon's unique philosophical take on hunches with vivid analogy. The sneeze comparison is unexpectedly perfect.

jokes.text: What if she had a hunch about Pearl Harbor? Is she supposed to keep that to herself, or should she tell her husband? 'Franklin, I have a very bad feeling that something awful is going to happen at Pearl Harbor tomorrow. You must get the ships out.'
jokes.setup: Debate about sharing hunches
jokes.punchline: Larry escalates to Eleanor Roosevelt having psychic Pearl Harbor knowledge
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry absurd escalation to historical proportions. A bit verbose but the premise is solid.

jokes.text: 'Eleanor, you stupid twat.' 'Why didn't you say something?'
jokes.setup: Eleanor tells Franklin about her Pearl Harbor hunch after the fact
jokes.punchline: Franklin's harsh response to his wife's late revelation
jokes.explanation: The shock of hearing 'twat' applied to Eleanor Roosevelt by FDR creates the laugh. Economical and unexpected.

jokes.text: Know why? 'Cause your fucking shoulders didn't go up. Your shoulders didn't move. And that mannerism's only good for hunches and shit.
jokes.setup: Leon disputes Jeff having a hunch
jokes.punchline: Leon's specific physical criteria for authentic hunches - shoulder movement
jokes.explanation: Leon's hyper-specific physical requirements for hunches is brilliantly absurd. The shoulder criterion is unexpectedly detailed and funny.

jokes.text: Actually, those are made up deli owner characters. My name is Lorenzo.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if he's Ollie or Al
jokes.punchline: Restaurant owner reveals the generic deli names are fictional
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment about generic restaurant naming conventions. Understated but effective.

jokes.text: You know what they say, the darker the berry, the sweeter the juice. Darker the berry, meaning the darker you are, the sweeter your juice is.
jokes.setup: Leon commenting on berries being delicious
jokes.punchline: Leon applies berry saying to racial implications
jokes.explanation: Leon's interpretation of the saying. A bit obvious but delivered with characteristic directness.

jokes.text: If a cannibal ate me, that motherfucker would be like, 'Mm! Wow, this motherfucker's delicious.'
jokes.setup: Leon's theory about darker people tasting better
jokes.punchline: Leon imagines a cannibal's enthusiastic reaction to eating him
jokes.explanation: Leon's dark turn to cannibalism is unexpected and delivered with his signature confidence. The sound effect adds impact.

jokes.text: I think he's got a point, 'cause Dahmer mostly ate Black people, did he not?
jokes.setup: Leon's cannibal theory
jokes.punchline: Larry provides disturbing historical support via Jeffrey Dahmer
jokes.explanation: Larry's factual backing of Leon's absurd theory is characteristically inappropriate and funny.

jokes.text: You start walking like that, man, they gotta give you your own fucking lane. That way it won't throw regular motherfuckers off.
jokes.setup: Larry's awkward walking style
jokes.punchline: Leon's traffic management solution for Larry's walk
jokes.explanation: Leon's practical urban planning approach to Larry's weird walk is characteristically logical yet absurd.

jokes.text: And then they'll add you to the evolution of man chart. Like motherfuckers who walk normal, and then all of a sudden, at the end... Like modern day motherfuckers walk like this.
jokes.setup: Larry's walk could catch on
jokes.punchline: Leon envisions Larry as next evolutionary step in human locomotion
jokes.explanation: Leon's evolutionary theory about Larry's walk becoming humanity's future is brilliantly absurd escalation.

jokes.text: You're not a good jiggler.
jokes.setup: Larry struggling with zipper
jokes.punchline: Leon's assessment of Larry's jiggling technique
jokes.explanation: Leon's deadpan critique of Larry's zipper skills. Economy and timing make it work.

jokes.text: I'm in talks about this, um, Waze app feature. A movie, uh, based on the Waze app? Yeah. Ron Howard's people.
jokes.setup: Les explaining why he'll be busy
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous concept of a movie based on GPS navigation app
jokes.explanation: The absurdity of a Waze movie perfectly captures Hollywood's desperation for IP. Les's earnestness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You know what shingles means? It's herpes.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions Leon getting shingles vaccine
jokes.punchline: Les reveals shingles is herpes, creating alarm
jokes.explanation: Basic medical information delivered as shocking revelation. The timing and Les's delivery help.

jokes.text: I wouldn't get back in that car. Wait a fucking minute. It can kill men your age.
jokes.setup: Les's shingles revelation
jokes.punchline: Immediate panic and ageist mortality warning
jokes.explanation: Escalation of panic over non-existent threat. Leon's reaction adds to the chaos.

jokes.text: Let's just say it's a very delicate matter where nothing is left to the imagination. There's an old saying at home, 'Every hole is a goal.'
jokes.setup: Les explaining what a 'complete clear out' is
jokes.punchline: Overly graphic description followed by crude British saying
jokes.explanation: Les's oversharing about colonics with crude aphorism is peak cringe comedy. The saying is memorable.

jokes.text: Hey, go fuck yourself, Crabby.
jokes.setup: Les finally leaves after the awkward encounter
jokes.punchline: Leon's hostile goodbye using Les's surname as insult
jokes.explanation: Leon's direct hostility combined with the name pun is perfectly in character and economical.

jokes.text: Feeble motherfuckers don't even know they fucking feeble.
jokes.setup: Larry asking if he's enfeebled
jokes.punchline: Leon's theory about self-awareness and physical decline
jokes.explanation: Leon's philosophical observation about unconscious incompetence regarding aging is both funny and unfortunately true.

jokes.text: You know how many times I've had to throw my pants on real quick? And the lady's husband shows up or her boyfriend shows up, I gotta put that motherfucker on quick. I never derail. You got zipper karma.
jokes.setup: Leon claiming he can zip things up quickly
jokes.punchline: Reveals this skill comes from escaping jealous partners, introduces 'zipper karma'
jokes.explanation: Leon's casual revelation about his adultery escape skills and philosophical concept of 'zipper karma' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: It's called, 'Don't go there.'
jokes.setup: Leon asking about her lipstick color
jokes.punchline: Lipstick 'name' that's actually a warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect response to Leon's flirtation. The fake lipstick name doubles as a direct rejection.

jokes.text: In that bungalow I got back there, I had a lot of hit and runs. You know what I'm saying? So, if you ever wanna get in an accident, that could be, uh, T-boned, sideswiped, head on, or rear ended. You feel me?
jokes.setup: Leon talking to Larry's lawyer
jokes.punchline: Elaborate car crash metaphor for sexual positions
jokes.explanation: Leon's elaborate car accident metaphor for sex is creative, inappropriate, and perfectly delivered to a professional woman.

jokes.text: Like a mortician. Like you 'bout to bury a motherfucker.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Larry's formal courtroom attire
jokes.punchline: Leon's assessment that Larry looks like an undertaker
jokes.explanation: Leon's visual assessment is vivid and characteristically direct. The imagery works well.

jokes.text: That's not a woman. That's my ex-wife.
jokes.setup: Leon defending Cheryl as 'a woman'
jokes.punchline: Larry's distinction between women and ex-wives
jokes.explanation: Larry's bitter distinction about ex-wives not counting as women is characteristically awful and funny.

jokes.text: Maybe I should go over there and talk to her and make sure she's even qualified to be giving hand jobs to people.
jokes.setup: Discussing how to handle Chunhua situation
jokes.punchline: Larry's oblivious suggestion to investigate her qualifications
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete obliviousness to how this would make things worse is peak Curb logic.

jokes.text: And if Chunhua's gon' be there, then maybe Bruce Springsteen would like a hand job.
jokes.setup: Larry planning to introduce Chunhua to Bruce
jokes.punchline: Leon's suggestion that Bruce might want her services
jokes.explanation: Leon's practical application of the misunderstanding to help Bruce. Callback to earlier confusion.

jokes.text: He changed his rating from a C to an A. You're kidding me. And you saw him do it?
jokes.setup: Discussion about restaurant health ratings
jokes.punchline: Larry's shock at witnessing health grade fraud
jokes.explanation: Sets up Larry's moral outrage about restaurant fraud. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I don't know if it's full-tilt squealing, but it's... it's squealish.
jokes.setup: Debate about whether reporting restaurant is squealing
jokes.punchline: Larry's careful distinction about levels of squealing
jokes.explanation: Larry's invented word 'squealish' and his careful moral calibration is characteristically neurotic and funny.

jokes.text: I used to be Kendra. And now, I'm Ken. Remember Kendra? Kendra Morris? And now, I'm Ken.
jokes.setup: Ken introducing himself to the group
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's Larry's former sexual partner who has transitioned
jokes.explanation: Major cringe moment as Ken reveals past relationship. The awkwardness is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Larry and I used to hook up. Let's just say it. Let's just say it.
jokes.setup: Ken has revealed his past identity
jokes.punchline: Ken insists on publicizing their former sexual relationship
jokes.explanation: Ken's insistence on transparency while Larry wants to avoid the topic escalates the discomfort.

jokes.text: I didn't make you out for a floor fucker.
jokes.setup: Ken revealing Larry liked sex on the floor
jokes.punchline: Bruce's surprised assessment of Larry's sexual preferences
jokes.explanation: Bruce Springsteen saying 'floor fucker' is inherently hilarious. The contrast between his status and the crude term is perfect.

jokes.text: Because if you have sex on the floor, nobody wants to hang out on the floor afterwards, so you can, you know, put your coat on and go home.
jokes.setup: Being asked why he liked floor sex
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical but cold reasoning for avoiding intimacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - practical, selfish, and emotionally tone-deaf. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Who wants to talk to somebody you've had sex with?
jokes.setup: Explaining his post-coital strategy
jokes.punchline: Larry's rhetorical question about post-sex conversation
jokes.explanation: Larry's awful question delivered as if it's obvious common sense. Peak Larry selfishness presented as logic.

jokes.text: I slept with Kendra not Ken. Kendra is not Ken. Kendra's not Ken. I am Ken. Ken is Kendra. Kendra is Ken. Ken is Ken, Kendra is Kendra, and never the twain shall meet.
jokes.setup: Debate about Ken's identity and Larry's past
jokes.punchline: Increasingly frantic identity confusion with literary reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of confusion with Larry's desperate Kipling reference. The verbal chaos perfectly captures the discomfort.

jokes.text: Oh, we've met. I'm Ken, and we banged it out.
jokes.setup: Larry's 'never the twain shall meet' line
jokes.punchline: Ken's crude contradiction with direct sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Ken's blunt response destroys Larry's literary pretension with crude reality. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: That is not introduction. But no handshake, no talk, not even picture! I never said anything about a picture. I said I'd introduce you. I introduced you. No introduction! You fail! You fail!
jokes.setup: Larry's minimal introduction to Bruce
jokes.punchline: Chunhua's detailed critique of what constitutes proper introduction
jokes.explanation: Chunhua's specific requirements for introductions vs Larry's literal interpretation. Good escalation.

jokes.text: I have COVID. You do? Yeah. I had a little sore throat, so I figured I'd take a test. What'd it say? Positive.
jokes.setup: Jeff calling Larry
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact COVID diagnosis delivery
jokes.explanation: Setup for larger COVID plot. Functional dialogue that moves story forward.

jokes.text: Maybe I was wrong about whose glass was whose?
jokes.setup: News that Bruce has COVID after their dinner
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic admission about the water glass mix-up
jokes.explanation: Larry's rare admission of error, delivered sarcastically. Good callback to earlier confidence.

jokes.text: It's too bad I didn't have more social obligations. It's such a great excuse.
jokes.setup: Larry has COVID and is quarantining
jokes.punchline: Larry's regret about not having more things to cancel
jokes.explanation: Larry finding the silver lining in illness as excuse to avoid people is perfectly in character and relatable.

jokes.text: Yeah, I feel like a pariah. More than normal.
jokes.setup: Larry discussing his COVID status
jokes.punchline: Larry's admission he's usually a pariah anyway
jokes.explanation: Larry's self-aware acknowledgment of his normal social status. Perfect economy and character insight.

jokes.text: I don't know what that shit gonna do to my dick.
jokes.setup: Leon not wanting to get COVID
jokes.punchline: Leon's specific concern about COVID's effect on his penis
jokes.explanation: Leon's priorities about COVID effects. Characteristically crude and self-focused.

jokes.text: Like whooping cough, scurvy. You know what I mean? Rickets and shit like that.
jokes.setup: Leon thought Larry caught 'old people' diseases
jokes.punchline: Leon's list of antiquated diseases he expected Larry to get
jokes.explanation: Leon's expectation that Larry would get historically appropriate old-timey diseases. Good character logic.

jokes.text: You can't hit it and quit it with your wife.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Larry's floor sex strategy
jokes.punchline: Leon's assessment that the strategy doesn't work in marriage
jokes.explanation: Leon's practical relationship wisdom delivered crudely. Good economy and callback.

jokes.text: 'Hey, how 'bout the way Breadman's playing? The way he controls that puck, it's really amazing, isn't it?'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining what post-sex talk he'd prefer
jokes.punchline: Larry's fantasy of hockey discussion instead of emotions
jokes.explanation: Larry's specific sports talk preference over intimacy shows his emotional avoidance perfectly. The detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I was in a three-way, shit got messy, and I got 'We Too-ed.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about Me Too movement
jokes.punchline: Leon's variation for group situations - 'We Too'
jokes.explanation: Leon's wordplay innovation for group Me Too situations is brilliant and economical. Perfect character logic.

jokes.text: Know what's even better? Fucking in mud.
jokes.setup: Discussion about floor sex
jokes.punchline: Leon's even more extreme sexual venue preference
jokes.explanation: Leon's escalation to even more uncomfortable sexual locations. Characteristically outrageous.

jokes.text: You're a walking fucking virus, Larry! Let me tell you something! You're the virus! Injecting yourself into everybody's lives! You're the virus! You're the virus!
jokes.setup: Susie angry about Larry giving Bruce COVID
jokes.punchline: Susie's metaphorical virus accusation about Larry's meddling nature
jokes.explanation: Susie's brilliant metaphor extending virus to Larry's personality. The repetition builds intensity perfectly.

jokes.text: I have a fantastic aura! Jeff, don't I have a good aura? A great aura. I have a great... It's an epic aura. I have a Hall of Fame aura.
jokes.setup: Susie attacking Larry's aura
jokes.punchline: Larry's increasingly grandiose self-assessment of his aura
jokes.explanation: Larry's escalating claims about his aura quality show his defensiveness and narcissism. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: I would rather be all those things than the tattletale you are. You had to tell Cheryl I like to have sex on the floor?
jokes.setup: Susie calling Larry small and petty
jokes.punchline: Larry's counter-accusation about Susie's tattling
jokes.explanation: Larry's deflection and callback to earlier revelation. Shows his priorities and grievances.

jokes.text: You cold-hearted, COVID-carrying cocksucker!
jokes.setup: Final exchange in Larry/Susie fight
jokes.punchline: Susie's alliterative insult combining his diseases
jokes.explanation: Susie's alliterative finale perfectly caps their fight. The rhythm and wordplay make it memorable.

jokes.text: Somebody in this house order massage. Open gate or I leave, and you pay me.
jokes.setup: Security guard saying no massage was ordered
jokes.punchline: Chunhua's threat about payment for unrendered services
jokes.explanation: Chunhua's aggressive sales technique and payment threat. Shows her determination and cunning.

jokes.text: Bruce, only me and reflection. No, Bruce, only me and reflection. Take photo! Take photo, you dumb, bald man.
jokes.setup: Chunhua getting photo with Bruce
jokes.punchline: Chunhua's critique of photo quality and insult to Larry
jokes.explanation: Chunhua's perfectionism about her Bruce photo and casual insult to Larry. Shows her priorities.

jokes.text: You gave Bruce COVID, you psycho! You ruined the tour. I had front row seats. It was my last chance to see him.
jokes.setup: Fan recognizing Larry on the street
jokes.punchline: Fan's personal attack about ruined concert experience
jokes.explanation: Fan's personal grievance shows Larry's public consequences. Escalates his pariah status.

jokes.text: One of my guys had sex with a waitress on the floor. Health department found out, and then, boom, we got a C.
jokes.setup: Lorenzo explaining the C rating
jokes.punchline: Reveals restaurant employee floor sex caused health violation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback connecting Larry's floor sex to restaurant health violations. Great coincidence.

jokes.text: What kind of asshole doesn't want to cuddle after sex? That's what separates us from animals. We're not two badgers fucking in the woods.
jokes.setup: Discussion about floor sex avoiding intimacy
jokes.punchline: Lorenzo's passionate defense of post-coital cuddling with animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Lorenzo's passionate moral stance and vivid animal imagery create a great counterpoint to Larry's coldness.

jokes.text: There's never an excuse not to cuddle. That guy is a straight up sick fuck.
jokes.setup: Lorenzo's cuddling requirements
jokes.punchline: Lorenzo's absolute moral condemnation of non-cuddlers
jokes.explanation: Lorenzo's absolute moral stance and harsh judgment. The passion and certainty make it funny.

jokes.text: I only did it in my house. He's doing it in restaurants.
jokes.setup: Lorenzo condemning restaurant floor sex
jokes.punchline: Larry's attempt to distinguish his floor sex as more appropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - trying to claim moral high ground on floor sex venue appropriateness. Brilliant character moment.

jokes.text: It's sad to think that there might be a guy out there who has sex on the floor... And I'm never gonna meet him.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the restaurant employee
jokes.punchline: Larry's wistful longing to meet a fellow floor sex enthusiast
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - finding kinship with someone he's never met based on shared sexual quirk. Beautiful character writing.

tmdb_overview: A misunderstanding with Cheryl's masseuse threatens Larry's image. Then, public perception of Larry sinks even lower when he gives the wrong person COVID.
